text:
🔭 Spec-driven development — what it is

SDD makes a formal, version-controlled spec the source of truth; code is a build artifact derived from it. Emerged 2025 as the antidote to "vibe coding" — LLM agents that drift from intent when prompts are underspecified.

Core loop: write spec (goals + constraints + acceptance criteria + edge cases) → generate & validate implementation → update spec when requirements change.

Key points:
• vs TDD/BDD: TDD = tests first (unit level); BDD = behavior first; SDD = spec first, anchors everything including tests + docs for AI agents. [Practitioner consensus]
• "Intent drift" is the failure mode SDD solves: prompting "add login" lets the model pick reasonable defaults that differ from intent. [Fact / Thoughtworks 2025]
• Multi-agent pattern: Coordinator breaks spec → Implementor sub-agents get sub-specs → Verifier checks output vs original spec. [Fact / Augment Code]
• AWS Kiro (May 2026): agentic IDE with built-in SDD pipeline: Requirements → Design → Tasks → Code. [Fact / AWS Builder Center]
• For PMs: spec authorship IS the PM role in AI-native teams. Relevant to SoftServe agentic-AI work + VP Product positioning.
